title: Schoenus riparius
intro: Schoenus riparius, also known as marsh club-rush, is a perennial aquatic plant that grows in wetlands and marshy areas. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues and skin infections. Bioactive constituents of Schoenus riparius include essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Schoenus riparius include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support immune function and promote overall well-being.
uses: Schoenus riparius is known to have been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. It is traditionally used in some regions to treat skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues. The plant's medicinal uses are still not well-documented and require further investigation.
